name: Herb Crusted Rack of Lamb with Roasted
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup extra virgin olive oil (1/2 to 3/4)
recipeInstructions:
Preparation of the herb crust:
Chop all herbs and blend with other ingredients in a food processor until smooth. Store in refrigerator or freezer.
Preparation of the pepper ragout:
Preheat oven to 300°F (150°C).
Prelight a mesquite grill, let embers cool. Place peppers, chiles, and onion on grill and char well. Roast garlic cloves in foil pouch over coals until lightly golden brown. Steam peppers for ten minutes, remove skins, seeds, and membranes. Cut into large chunks.
Mix with chopped garlic. In a heavy saucepot, heat olive oil and sweat the pepper mixture. Add lavender, salt, pepper, and honey; bake in oven until liquid reduces. Cool to room temperature, adjust seasonings, and add basil leaves.
Preparation of the socca nicoise:
In a mixing bowl, combine flour with milk in steady streams until smooth. Add olive oil, egg, salt, and pepper. Let rest for two hours in refrigerator. Consistency should be like crepe batter.
Preparation of the tapenade:
Puree ingredients in food processor; keep chunky. Let sit at room temperature.
Presentation:
Preheat oven to 450°F (230°C).
Heat a heavy saute pan with olive oil over high heat until smoking. Season lamb and sear on all sides, placing in oven for fifteen minutes until rare. Place herb butter on rack and return to oven for five to seven minutes until medium rare and crust is golden brown. Let rest for five minutes.
Cut rack into halves between chops and keep warm. In a nonstick pan, heat olive oil and color chick pea batter well. Serve with roasted pepper ragout in center, top with lamb, and drizzle with tapenade. Garnish with parsley. Serve immediately.
Yield: 4 servings.
